name: square-mcp-server
title: Square (Official Meta-Tool)
description: Square's official MCP server. Single meta-tool (`make_request`) covering the full Square REST surface — Payments, Customers, Orders, Catalog, Inventory, Disputes, Bookings, Loyalty, Gift Cards, Team. Breadth at the cost of token efficiency.
tagline: Full Square REST surface via a single meta-tool
author: square

env.name: ACCESS_TOKEN
env.description: Square personal access token from Developer Dashboard

setup.help_text: Create a personal access token at Square → Developer Dashboard → Applications. Defaults to sandbox; set SANDBOX=false to point at production.
setup.blurb: Square's official MCP server. Single meta-tool (`make_request`) covering the full Square REST surface — Payments, Customers, Orders, Catalog, Inventory, Disputes, Bookings, Loyalty, Gift Cards, Team. Breadth at the cost of token efficiency.

highlights.text: Full breadth — covers every public Square REST endpoint
Single tool — `make_request` parses path/method/body at the model side
Schema discovery — `get_service_info` and `get_type_info` document the surface on demand
Always current — auto-tracks new Square API releases without server updates
Token-heavy — full REST responses, no projection, no formatting
Escape hatch — useful for endpoints not covered by typed-tool blades

readme:
### What it does

Square's official MCP server exposes the entire Square REST surface through a single meta-tool, `make_request`. The agent constructs the path, method, and body itself, with help from two discovery tools (`get_service_info`, `get_type_info`) that surface schema info on demand.

This covers the **full** API — Payments, Refunds, Customers, Cards, Orders, Catalog, Inventory, Disputes, Webhooks, Bookings, Loyalty, Gift Cards, Team, Locations, Devices — without any blade-side curation.

### When to use this vs `square-blade-mcp`

- **Use `square-blade-mcp`** for everyday Payments, Refunds, Customers, Cards, Orders, Catalog, Inventory, Disputes, Webhooks. You get 30 typed tools, formatted output, write/confirm gates, PCI-safe card display, credential scrubbing, and `payments-v1` contract conformance.
- **Use `square-mcp-server`** as an escape hatch when you need an endpoint the blade does not expose — Bookings, Loyalty, Gift Cards, Team, Devices, beta APIs.

Both servers can be installed side-by-side. Routing and tool gating are managed by the harness.

### Trade-offs

The meta-tool pattern is breadth-optimal but token-heavy. Each call returns full REST JSON with all nested objects, dates, IDs, and metadata. Card details include the full PAN if present in the response. There is no write gate, no confirmation step, and no credential scrubbing on errors.

For production agentic workflows on Payments, prefer `square-blade-mcp`.

scenarios.label: Manage Square Bookings appointments
scenarios.body: Use the meta-tool to list, create, or cancel Bookings appointments — an area not covered by the typed `square-blade-mcp` blade.

scenarios.label: Inspect Loyalty programs
scenarios.body: Query Loyalty program configuration, accounts, and rewards via `make_request` — an escape-hatch for endpoints outside the payments-v1 contract.

scenarios.label: Issue and redeem Gift Cards
scenarios.body: Create gift cards, link them to customers, query balances, and process redemptions through the Gift Cards REST endpoints.

notes: Square's first-party MCP server. Single-tool meta-pattern (`make_request`) covers the full REST surface. Listed as community/escape-hatch — for typed payments-v1 conformance use square-blade-mcp instead.
